title: Designing for Million-Token Windows
concept: Combining FlashAttention, RoPE scaling, and sparse attention enables modern models to process entire books in one prompt. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Multimodal LLMs.'

options.text: The speed at which the AI types answers
The amount of text the AI can read and remember in one prompt
The size of the AI application's window on your screen

exercises.explain: A context window is the total amount of text (in tokens) that an LLM can read and process in a single prompt.

cards.title: The Ultimate Memory Span
cards.body: Have you ever tried to read a whole textbook in just one second? Traditional AI models used to forget the beginning of a document by the time they reached the end. Today, we design models that can read and remember entire libraries in one go!

cards.title: Million-Token Windows
cards.body: A million-token window is the ability of an AI model to hold massive amounts of data—like a whole Harry Potter series—in its active memory at the same time. This lets you ask questions about any detail across the entire text instantly.

cards.title: The Library Desk
cards.body: Think of a normal LLM like a desk that can only hold one sheet of paper at a time. If you want to read a new page, you have to throw the old one away. A million-token window is like a massive library table where you can spread out hundreds of books all at once, looking at everything simultaneously without losing your place.

cards.title: Analyzing Entire Archives
cards.body: Imagine uploading a 1,000-page financial report and a 500-page competitor analysis to an AI. Instead of reading summary snippets, the AI analyzes both documents in full detail to spot hidden trends instantly.

cards.title: Myth: Just Get Bigger Computers
cards.body: Many believe that to process a million tokens, AI models just need bigger computers. In reality, hardware alone isn't enough; we need smart algorithms like FlashAttention and RoPE scaling to prevent the AI from slowing to a crawl.

cards.title: Short Context vs. Million-Token Window
cards.body: Let's compare traditional short-context models with modern million-token designs.

left.text: Can only read small snippets. Forgets the beginning of a document when it gets to the end.

right.text: Uses FlashAttention and RoPE to process entire books at once without forgetting anything.

exercises.prompt: Which combination of techniques allows modern LLMs to scale up to million-token context windows?

options.text: Combining FlashAttention, RoPE scaling, and sparse attention

exercises.explain: FlashAttention, RoPE position scaling, and sparse attention are key architectural ingredients that allow models to stretch their memory windows to millions of tokens.

exercises.prompt: True or False: With a million-token context window, an LLM can analyze a complete novel in a single prompt without losing track of early chapters.
exercises.explain: A million-token window allows the model to process all the pages at the exact same time, retaining all details without summarization.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing hardware-saving technique:
exercises.sentence: To keep memory usage low while reading a whole book, LLMs rely on _____ to speed up memory access.
exercises.explain: FlashAttention speeds up calculations and saves memory, making it possible for the AI to handle massive inputs.

exercises.prompt: Which AI explains the design behind million-token windows correctly?

options.text: We achieve million-token windows by using traditional attention mechanisms and simply adding more CPU cores to store the words.
We achieve million-token windows by using FlashAttention to save memory, RoPE to handle long position codes, and sparse attention to focus on key parts.
We achieve million-token windows by translating all input texts into short summaries before feeding them to the AI model.

exercises.explain: AI Option 2 is correct because it highlights RoPE scaling, FlashAttention, and sparse attention as the combined techniques enabling massive context windows.

exercises.prompt: How do modern LLMs manage to process million-token windows without running out of computer memory?

options.text: By making the model write shorter answers
By using FlashAttention, RoPE scaling, and sparse attention together
By training the model on only one book at a time
By disabling the attention mechanism completely

exercises.explain: By combining FlashAttention (speed), RoPE scaling (stretching positions), and sparse attention (focusing on what matters), LLMs can read entire libraries in a single breath! Next up: Multimodal LLMs.
